dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7002 IH: Justice for Exploited Children Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Scholten (for herself, Mr. Mackenzie, Mr. Goldman of New York, Mr. Thanedar, Mr. Landsman, Mr. Krishnamoorthi, Ms. Norton, and Ms. Titus) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To amend the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to provide for enhanced penalties for child labor violations.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Justice for Exploited Children Act.

subsection.text: Section 16(a) of the Fair Labor Standards Act (29 U.S.C. 216(a)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking Any and inserting (1) Any;
by striking this subsection each place it appears and inserting this paragraph; and
by adding at the end the following:
Any person who repeatedly or willfully violates section 15(a)(4) shall upon conviction thereof be subject to a fine of not more than $100,000, or to imprisonment for not more than 5 years, or both.
Any person shall upon conviction for a violation of section 15(a)(4) be subject to a fine of not more than $500,000, or to imprisonment for not more than 10 years, or both, if such violation was—

subparagraph.text: willful and resulted in the death or serious injury of an employee under the age of 18 years; or
repeated and such violation and a prior violation each resulted in the death or serious injury of such an employee.

subsection.text: Section 16(e) of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (29 U.S.C. 216(e)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking not to exceed— and inserting as follows:;

subparagraph.text: by moving the margins for clauses (i) and (ii) 4 ems to the left;

clause.text: by striking $11,000 and inserting Not less than $1,000 but not more than $150,000; and
by striking ; or and inserting , which penalty shall be doubled where the violation is a repeated or willful violation.;
by striking $50,000 and inserting Not less than $25,000 but not more than $601,150;
by striking may and inserting shall; and

subparagraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: Not less than $50,000 and not more than $601,150 with regard to each such violation that causes the death of any employee under the age of 18 years, which penalty shall be doubled where the violation is a repeated or willful violation.

paragraph.text: in paragraph (B), by striking subparagraph (A) and inserting this section.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this Act shall apply with respect to violations occurring on or after the date of enactment of this Act.
